No Poverty.reason: The course syllabus contains no explicit or implicit evidence related to poverty eradication, social protection, or economic resources for the poor.

Zero Hunger.reason: The course syllabus provides no evidence related to hunger, food security, nutrition, or sustainable agriculture practices.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: There is no mention in the course syllabus of health, well-being, disease prevention, or access to healthcare services.

Quality Education.reason: The course is a university seminar preparing students for graduate studies (Target 4.3). It explicitly covers '永續揭露準則' and 'ESG 研究', fostering skills for sustainable development (Target 4.7).
evidence: understand and be able to identify the ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability considerations

Gender Equality.reason: The course syllabus lacks any specific content or learning outcomes related to gender equality, women's empowerment, or discrimination.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No evidence in the syllabus pertains to water quality, sanitation, hygiene, or water resource management.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: While '碳會計' (carbon accounting) is mentioned, the course does not explicitly address energy efficiency, renewable energy, or access to clean energy services (Target 7.3). The link is too indirect.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course provides professional accounting/auditing skills for decent jobs (Target 8.5), covers sustainability standards (indirectly supporting Target 8.4), and discusses financial industry auditing (Target 8.10).
evidence: 習得會計與稅務相關 專業領域知識
保險業與一般金融業的審計議題

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course's focus on '永續揭露準則' and '碳會計' enables industries to report on sustainable upgrades and resource efficiency (Target 9.4), and fosters research capabilities (Target 9.5).
evidence: 提升學生對於財務報導與審計最新發展的掌握程度

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The syllabus does not contain explicit content on income inequality, social inclusion, or non-discriminatory policies, despite the general nature of financial regulation.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No evidence in the course syllabus relates to urban planning, housing, transport, cultural heritage, or disaster resilience in communities.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: The course directly addresses Target 12.6 by teaching '永續揭露準則(IFRS S1/S2)', which are standards for companies to integrate sustainability information into their reporting cycle, and introduces ESG research.
evidence: Students will understand and be able to identify the ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability considerations

Climate Action.reason: The course directly supports climate action (Targets 13.2, 13.3) by teaching '永續揭露準則與碳會計' (IFRS S1/S2), which are key tools for corporate climate reporting and awareness.
evidence: 永續揭露準則與碳會計 (IFRS S1/S2)
說明 S1 與 S2 架構與內容

Life Below Water.reason: The course syllabus contains no evidence related to marine ecosystems, ocean pollution, or sustainable fisheries.

Life on Land.reason: There is no mention in the syllabus of terrestrial ecosystems, forests, biodiversity, or land degradation.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The study of accounting and auditing standards (TWSA 701) directly supports the development of accountable and transparent institutions (Target 16.6) and helps reduce corruption (Target 16.5).
evidence: 新頒布會計與審計準則之研討
審計意見沿革與相關學術研究
權威機構的審計品質定義

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course's focus on international standards (IFRS 17, 18, S1/S2) enhances policy coherence (Target 17.14) and builds capacity for sustainable development reporting (Target 17.19), fostering an international perspective.
